title: Sarga 105
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “While those two were thus conversing, the revered sage Durvasa, desiring an audience with Rama, approached the royal gate.” through “...then I shall curse your kingdom, you, the city, Rama, and also Bharata, O son of Sumitra, and all your progeny.” through “Having heard those words spoken by Rama, the best of sages, Durvasa, replied to the lord, saying, "O lover of righteousness, listen."”

Themes: mind and meditation, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “O sinless one, today marks the completion of my thousand-year penance, and so I desire whatever food is ready to break my fast.” through “After that excellent sage had returned to his hermitage, Rama, the elder brother of Lakshmana, remembered the words of Kala and was then…” through “Then, the greatly renowned Rama, having intellectually ascertained the words of Kala and deciding that "this is not (to be avoided)", remained…”

verses.sanskrit: तथा तयोः संवदतोर्दुर्वासा भगवानृषिः । रामस्य दर्शनाकाङ्क्षी राजद्वारमुपागमत् ।। 7.105.1 ।।
verses.translation: While those two were thus conversing, the revered sage Durvasa, desiring an audience with Rama, approached the royal gate.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽभिगम्य तु सौमित्रिमुवाच ऋषिसत्तमः । रामं दर्शय मे शीघ्रं पुरा मे ऽर्थो ऽतिवर्तते ।। 7.105.2 ।।
verses.translation: Having approached Saumitri, the best of sages indeed said, "Show Rama to me quickly, before my purpose passes away."

verses.sanskrit: मुनेस्तु भाषितं श्रुत्वा लक्ष्मणः परवीरहा । अभिवाद्य महात्मानं वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ।। 7.105.3 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the words of the sage, Lakshmana, the destroyer of enemy heroes, saluted the great-souled one and then spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: किं कार्यं ब्रूहि भगवन्को वा ऽर्थः किं करोम्यहम् । व्यग्रो हि राघवो ब्रह्मन्मुहूर्तं प्रतिपाल्यताम् ।। 7.105.4 ।।
verses.translation: Lakshmana asked, "O revered one, what is the task or purpose, and what shall I do, for Rama is indeed busy, O Brahmana, please wait for a moment."

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा ऋषिशार्दूलः क्रोधेन कलुषीकृतः । उवाच लक्ष्मणं वाक्यं निर्दहन्निव चक्षुषा ।। 7.105.5 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that, the tiger among sages, agitated by anger, spoke words to Lakshmana as if burning him with his eyes.

verses.sanskrit: अस्मिन्क्षणे मां सौमित्रे रामाय प्रतिवेदय । अस्मिन्क्षणे मां सौमित्रे न निवेदयसे यदि ।। 7.105.6 ।।
verses.translation: The sage commanded, "O son of Sumitra, announce me to Rama this very moment; if you do not announce me to Rama this very moment, O son of Sumitra..."

verses.sanskrit: विषयं त्वां पुरं चैव शपिष्ये राघवं तथा । भरतं चैव सौमित्रे युष्माकं या च सन्ततिः ।। 7.105.7 ।।
verses.translation: ...then I shall curse your kingdom, you, the city, Rama, and also Bharata, O son of Sumitra, and all your progeny.

verses.sanskrit: न हि शक्ष्याम्यहं भूयो मन्युं धारयितुं हृदि । तच्छ्रुत्वा घोरसङ्काशं वाक्यं तस्य महात्मनः । चिन्तयामास मनसा तस्य वाक्यस्य निश्चयम् ।। 7.105.8 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the terrible words of that great soul, he pondered in his mind the definite meaning of that statement, realizing he could no longer contain his anger.

verses.sanskrit: एकस्य मरणं मे ऽस्तु मा भूत्सर्वविनाशनम् । इति बुद्ध्या विनिश्चित्य राघवाय न्यवेदयत् ।। 7.105.9 ।।
verses.translation: Deciding with his intellect that it was better for one person to die than for everyone to be destroyed, he reported this to Rama.

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणस्य वचः श्रुत्वा रामः कालं विसृज्य च । निस्सृत्य त्वरितं राजा अत्रेः पुत्रं ददर्श ह ।। 7.105.10 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Lakshmana's words, King Rama dismissed Kala and quickly came out to see the son of Atri.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽभिवाद्य महात्मानं ज्वलन्तमिव तेजसा । किं कार्यमिति काकुत्स्थः कृताञ्जलिरभाषत ।। 7.105.11 ।।
verses.translation: Having saluted the great soul who was blazing with splendor, Rama, with folded hands, asked what his purpose was.

verses.sanskrit: तद्वाक्यं राघवेणोक्तं श्रुत्वा मुनिवरः प्रभुम् । प्रत्याह रामं दुर्वासाः श्रूयतां धर्मवत्सल ।। 7.105.12 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard those words spoken by Rama, the best of sages, Durvasa, replied to the lord, saying, "O lover of righteousness, listen."

verses.sanskrit: अद्य वर्षसहस्रस्य समाप्तिस्तपसो मम । सो ऽहं भोजनमिच्छामि यथासिद्धं तवानघ ।। 7.105.13 ।।
verses.translation: O sinless one, today marks the completion of my thousand-year penance, and so I desire whatever food is ready to break my fast.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं राजा राघवः प्रीतमानसः । भोजनं मुनिमुख्याय यथासिद्धमुपाहरत् ।। 7.105.14 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that word, King Rama, with a pleased mind, offered food to the chief of sages as it was prepared.

verses.sanskrit: स तु भुक्त्वा मुनिश्रेष्ठस्तदन्नममृतोपमम् । साधु रामेति सम्भाष्य स्वमाश्रममुपागमत् ।। 7.105.15 ।।
verses.translation: Having eaten that nectar-like food, the best of sages praised Rama saying "Excellent, Rama!" and then returned to his own hermitage.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन् गते मुनिवरे स्वाश्रमं लक्ष्मणाग्रजः । संस्मृत्य कालवाक्यानि ततो दुःखमुपागमत् ।। 7.105.16 ।।
verses.translation: After that excellent sage had returned to his hermitage, Rama, the elder brother of Lakshmana, remembered the words of Kala and was then overcome with sorrow.

verses.sanskrit: दुःखेन च सुसन्तप्तः स्मृत्वा तद्घोरदर्शनम् । अवाङ्मुखो दीनमना व्याहर्तुं न शशाक ह ।। 7.105.17 ।।
verses.translation: Deeply distressed by sorrow and remembering that terrible sight, he became downcast and dejected, unable to utter a word.

verses.sanskrit: ततो बुद्ध्या विनिश्चित्य कालवाक्यानि राघवः । नैतदस्तीति निश्चित्य तूष्णीमासीन्महायशाः ।। 7.105.18 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the greatly renowned Rama, having intellectually ascertained the words of Kala and deciding that "this is not (to be avoided)", remained silent. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे पञ्चोत्तरशततमः सर्गः ।। 105 ।। Thus ends the hundred and fifth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
